core_concepts: Introduction to FDA laser product regulations
Federal Food, Drug and Cosmetic Act (FFDCA) Chapter V, Subchapter C

key_terms.definition: Federal Food, Drug and Cosmetic Act - primary legislation governing FDA authority
Regulatory framework for radiation-emitting electronic products
Code of Federal Regulations section containing laser product performance standard
Device producing electromagnetic radiation in wavelength range 180 nm to 1 x 10^6 nm by controlled stimulated emission
Periodic clarifications and policy updates issued by CDRH
Division of Small Manufacturers, International, and Consumer Assistance

learning_objectives: Understand the legal basis for FDA laser product regulation
Define what constitutes a laser under FDA regulations
Identify the regulatory hierarchy (Act → Regulations → Guidance)
Know where to seek regulatory assistance
prerequisites: Basic understanding of US regulatory structure
